sglang.title: Fix flaky optimistic prefill retry test
sglang.author: cctry
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
pop_bootstrapped finalized a WaitingForInput request unconditionally, so a should_force_retry request whose bootstrap had already completed skipped the optimistic-retry path entirely — making test_disaggregation_optimistic_prefill flaky (e.g. test_logprob: retry counter didn't increase).
Now honor s

sglang.title: [Spec] Unify decode KV-commit bookkeeping across spec-v2 workers
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Drop the bonus-slot pre-claim from EAGLE's prepare_for_decode. With no pre-claim, every spec-v2 worker commits the full accepted run uniformly in _resolve_spec_v2_tokens, so the is_dflash() fork there is deleted. kv_committed_len now honestly records "tokens in KV" across all workers.
Post-r

sglang.title: Re-enable SM90 FlashInfer allreduce fusion with safe backend defaults
sglang.author: mmangkad
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This follows up on PR #23402. I was working on a Hopper box and noticed FlashInfer allreduce fusion was not getting auto-enabled on SM90 anymore. Looking back at #23402, the last pre-merge change switched the model-specific auto-enable gate from SM90/SM10X to SM10X-only to get around Hopper

sglang.title: [HiCache] Fix hicache host memory leak by bounding PP-sync work_list
sglang.author: whybeyoung
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
The HiCache PP-sync path (#27285) enqueues an isend + cloned tensor into work_list every event round on PP0 without backpressure. When the downstream PP rank can't keep up (e.g. idle busy loop), work_list grows unbounded and leaks ~12GB/h.
Replace the non-blocking _reap_completed_async_work (run at

sglang.title: feat: session radix cache
sglang.author: ishandhanani
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Features
--enable-session-radix-cache makes a session a tag over ordinary evictable radix KV — no Session object, no pinned slot, no timeout.

A request carrying a session_id has its KV tagged in the radix cache — purely a release-grouping tag; the KV stays LRU-neutral and competes as ordinary radix

Motivation

Helios video generation does not run on Ascend NPU — the standard generate command crashes during inference due to two independent NPU-specific operator limitations. This PR fixes both so Helios runs end-to-end on Ascend. Both fixes are NPU-only.

sglang.title: [AMD] Fuse topk padded-token masking into a single Triton kernel
sglang.author: karverma-amd
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
On AMD HIP the aiter MoE kernels cannot use topk_ids=-1 to drop CUDA-graph padding rows, so padded tokens are masked by zeroing their routing weights instead. The eager arange + (>=) + boolean index_put_ path issues several launch-latency-bound kernels per call, twice per MoE layer.
Modifications

sglang.author: Raiden-Makoto
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The DeepSeek-V4 triton flashmla fp8 path assumed CUDA / gfx950 (CDNA4) e4m3fn, but
gfx942 (MI300 / MI325, CDNA3) uses e4m3fnuz. The two formats differ in exponent bias,
max value, and bit patterns, so on gfx942 the fp8 KV path collapses GSM8K accuracy
(~0.00) and emits NaNs in the sampler

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Model Runner V2] Preserve all allowed_token_ids in the logit bias kernel
vllm.author: Sunt-ing
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Under the V2 model runner, SamplingParams.allowed_token_ids can silently drop some of the allowed tokens. Generation then produces a token outside the allowlist, and when every allowed token happens to be dropped the whole logits row becomes -inf and the engine samples token id 0.
Root cause

Purpose
The Rust OpenAI frontend currently accepts allowed_token_ids: [] through the HTTP completions and chat completions paths. The request is lowered as Some(vec![]), and the existing token-id validation only checks for out-of-vocab IDs, so the empty list is accepted.
Python SamplingParams reject

vllm.title: fix gpt_oss pp>1 with ep
vllm.author: mayuyuace
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Bug: In GptOssModel.load_weights(), get_ep_group().rank returns the global rank across all workers, but the code uses it to slice expert weights as if it were the rank within the EP group.
With pipeline parallelism, PP stage 1 workers have global ranks offset by pp_rank * tp_size (e.g., ranks 2 and

vllm.title: [ROCm][Perf] DSv3.2: fuse MLA Q concat+fp8-quant in forward_mqa
vllm.author: frida-andersson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
On the ROCm sparse-MLA fp8-KV decode path, the query tensor was being built by two back-to-back HBM-bound kernels: ConcatMLAQKernel<bf16, 512> (concatenate q_nope + q_pe into bf16, ~5.2 µs) followed by scaled_fp8_quant (~5 µs). Both are pure copy-with-scale, no compute.
This PR routes forward_mqa th

vllm.title: fix(security): prevent infinite loop in split_audio with NaN audio sa…
vllm.author: jperezdealgaba
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
find_split_point returns 0 when all audio samples are NaN because NaN comparisons always evaluate to False under IEEE 754. T
his causes split_audio to never advance its loop cursor, permanently hanging the worker thread.
Fix by defaulting quietest_idx to start_idx instead of 0, skipping NaN energy v

vllm.title: [Kernel] Extend Marlin thread-tile padding to MoE (WNA16 + FP8/MXFP8)
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow-up to #45295, which added thread-tile padding to the dense Marlin paths and NVFP4 MoE. This extends the same mechanism to the remaining Marlin MoE preps: generic WNA16 (GPTQ/AWQ/compressed-tensors) and FP8/MXFP8.
TP sharding can leave the rank-local intermediate size off a Marlin thread tile.

NotImplementedError: No NvFp4 MoE backend supports the deployment configuration.

When swiglu_li

The TRTLLM NVFP4 fused MoE only supports Blackwell datacenter GPUs (capability family 100 / SM100), s

vllm.bodyExcerpt: Add an MXFP8 W8A8 linear GEMM that drives FlashInfer's mm_mxfp8(..., backend="cute-dsl"), sibling to the existing CUTLASS kernel. The cute-dsl backend consumes the same 1D swizzled F8_128x4 scales the CUTLASS path already produces, so weight/activation prep is identical and output is bit-identical;

vllm.title: [Bugfix][ToolParser] Handle braces in required tool streaming strings
vllm.author: Sunt-ing
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Related: #41111. In required tool_choice streaming, vLLM trims streamed tool-call JSON by counting the wrapper's bracket level. The counter currently treats { and } inside JSON string values as structural braces, so a valid generated argument such as {"city": "a } b"} can be emitted to the c

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Frontend] Emit non-ASCII tool-call arguments without \uXXXX escapes
vllm.author: EazyReal
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
The xlam, hunyuan_a13b, and seed_oss tool parsers serialize tool-call arguments with json.dumps(...) at its default ensure_ascii=True, so non-ASCII argument values (Chinese, emoji, ...) are emitted as \uXXXX escapes instead of literal UTF-8. This adds ensure_ascii=False at the argument-seria

vllm.title: [Optimization] Skip DP padding tokens in MoE
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
For DP/EP with CUDA graphs, a batch can contain a large number of padding tokens. Today, these padding tokens are handled the same way as real tokens, introducing unnecessary compute overhead.
For example, if one DP rank has a 512-token prefill while the other ranks are idle, every DP rank pads its

vllm.title: [Perf] Skip detokenization in online beam search
vllm.author: GuyStone
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
What & why
Online beam search builds an internal per-step SamplingParams requesting
logprobs = 2 * beam_width. The engine then detokenizes every one of those
2 * beam_width logprob token ids into strings on every decode step
(LogprobsProcessor → tokenizer.decode). Beam search never uses those
string

vllm.title: [Perf][DSv4/DSv3.2] Add cluster-cooperative topK kernel for low-latency scenarios
vllm.author: LopezCastroRoberto
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Adds a cluster-cooperative topK kernel for low-latency cases, which uses TMA and DSMEM, meaning that SM90+ arch is required. This new kernel has been extensively tuned to cover the whole low-latency regime (i.e. bs ≤32) with a cluster-level cooperation via cluster.sync() and distributed SMEM

main branch
